channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising Competition for Talent in Self-Driving Vehicle and Physical AI Sectors
item.description: The self-driving vehicle industry is experiencing a fierce competition for talent, with base salaries for engineers reaching $300,000 to $500,000, excluding equity and benefits. Startups and automaker...

item.title: Rory McIlroy secures sixth major with second consecutive Masters victory
item.description: Rory McIlroy successfully defended his Masters title at Augusta National, claiming his sixth major championship. Despite losing a six-shot lead on Saturday, McIlroy showed resilience and composure on ...

item.title: Canberra drone start-up Boresight expands into US defence industry
item.description: A Canberra-based defence start-up, Boresight, has achieved international success after beginning operations in a repurposed kitchen showroom in Fyshwick. The company specialises in low-cost, vividly c...

item.title: Arteta Faces Risk of Breaking Consecutive Second-Place Record After Bournemouth Loss
item.description: Mikel Arteta's Arsenal team suffered a frustrating 2-0 defeat to Bournemouth, raising concerns over their Premier League title ambitions. The loss marked Arsenal's third defeat in four games, and alth...

item.title: Rufa Mae Quinto impresses audiences with Katy Perry performance on Your Face Sounds Familiar 2026
item.description: During the latest episode of 'Your Face Sounds Familiar 2026,' Rufa Mae Quinto captivated the audience with an outstanding performance as she impersonated international pop star Katy Perry. Known for ...

item.title: Christine Frechette elected leader of CAQ and set to become Quebec's next premier
item.description: Christine Frechette has been elected as the new leader of the Coalition Avenir Québec (CAQ), defeating Bernard Drainville, and will assume the role of Quebec's premier. At 56 years old, she becomes th...

item.title: Why skills matter more than degrees in today’s job market
item.description: The article explains how the modern job market is changing its priorities, with employers increasingly looking beyond academic degrees and focusing more on practical skills, adaptability, and the abil...

item.title: Sundowns Claim Historic 1-0 Victory in Tunis Despite Playing with Ten Men
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns produced a resilient performance in Tunis on Sunday, claiming a 1-0 win over Esperance in the first leg of the CAF Champions League semi-final. Portuguese coach Miguel Cardoso, retur...

item.title: Billy Crawford Shares Memories from His Time on Your Face Sounds Familiar 2026
item.description: Billy Crawford, a prominent figure in Philippine entertainment, recently reflected on his experiences as part of the popular show 'Your Face Sounds Familiar' (YFSF) during its 2026 season. Throughout ...

item.title: PM raises concerns over security situation in Bengal amid political tensions
item.description: The recent statements by the Prime Minister have sparked discussions over the political climate in West Bengal. The PM has criticised the ruling Trinamool Congress party, suggesting that some of their...

item.title: Shield Curling Club claims first Rock League title after tight playoff finals
item.description: The first season of the professional Rock League concluded in Toronto with Shield Curling Club claiming the inaugural championship. In a thrilling final at Mattamy Athletic Centre, the Canadian-based ...

item.title: Washington Defines Key Limits in Iran Talks and Outlines Strategy to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: According to The Wall Street Journal, the United States has outlined several non-negotiable conditions—or red lines—for any potential agreement with Iran, as tensions in the Middle East continue to ri...

item.title: Ex-SK president fined for failing to account for government vehicle use
item.description: The Sandiganbayan First Division has convicted Emerson Dave Silutan, a former Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) president of Tagum City, for the lesser offense of 'failure of accountable officer to render acc...

item.title: African Countries with the Lowest IMF Debt by April 2026
item.description: As of April 2026, Namibia tops the list of African countries with the lowest IMF debt, having fully cleared its loans, demonstrating financial independence and control over its fiscal policies. Econom...

item.title: Inter Milan edge closer to Serie A crown after thrilling win over Como
item.description: Inter Milan don put one hand for Serie A trophy after dem show mad spirit to comeback from two goals down to beat Como 4-3 for a really exciting match. Como carry early advantage with goals from Alex ...

item.title: Military apprehends ISWAP teenage courier involved in Jilli area attacks
item.description: Troops from Operation Hadin Kai (OPHK) don arrest 15-year-old wey dey suspected to be ISWAP logistics courier for Ngamdu, Borno State. The pikin bin dey try collect supplies for insurgents wey dey ope...

item.title: Péter Magyar and Tisza end Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule with sweeping two-thirds majority in Hungary
item.description: Hungary witnessed a historic political shift on Sunday as Péter Magyar’s Tisza party defeated Viktor Orbán’s long-ruling Fidesz, marking the end of a 16-year conservative government. With nearly all v...

item.title: Brampton man charged following hit-and-run collision in Etobicoke
item.description: A man from Brampton is facing multiple charges after allegedly fleeing the scene of a hit-and-run collision in Etobicoke, Toronto. According to Toronto Police Service, the incident occurred in the Eto...

item.title: Toronto Raptors set for decisive regular-season finale against Brooklyn Nets
item.description: The Toronto Raptors are heading into their regular-season finale with nearly their entire roster available as they face the Brooklyn Nets in a game that will determine their playoff fate. According to...

item.title: Charl Schwartzel reflects on challenging Masters round and the need for mental rest
item.description: Charl Schwartzel, the 41-year-old South African golfer and 2011 Masters champion, expressed after his final round at Augusta National that he needs a mental break from the tournament. Starting as the ...
